exercises.title: SELECT All
exercises.description: Write a query to select all columns from the Customers table.

exercises.title: SELECT Specific Columns
exercises.description: Select only the CustomerName and Country columns from Customers.
exercises.hint: Use SELECT CustomerName, Country FROM Customers;
exercises.solutionCode: SELECT name, age FROM Users WHERE age > 25;

exercises.title: WHERE Clause
exercises.solutionCode: INSERT INTO Users (id, name, age) VALUES (3, 'Charlie', 28);

exercises.title: ORDER BY
exercises.description: Select all customers ordered by CustomerName in ascending order.
exercises.solutionCode: UPDATE Users SET age = 26 WHERE id = 1;

exercises.title: INSERT INTO
exercises.description: Insert a new customer: CustomerID 6, CustomerName 'New Corp', ContactName 'John Doe', Country 'USA'.
exercises.hint: Use INSERT INTO Customers VALUES (6, 'New Corp', 'John Doe', 'USA');

exercises.title: UPDATE
exercises.description: Update the country of the customer with CustomerID 1 to 'Austria'.
exercises.hint: Use UPDATE Customers SET Country = 'Austria' WHERE CustomerID = 1;

exercises.title: COUNT & GROUP BY
exercises.description: Count the number of customers in each Country. Select the country and the count.
exercises.hint: Use SELECT Country, COUNT(*) FROM Customers GROUP BY Country;
exercises.solutionCode: SELECT department, AVG(salary) FROM Employees GROUP BY department;

exercises.title: JOIN
exercises.description: Write an INNER JOIN between Customers and Orders on CustomerID. Select CustomerName and OrderID.
exercises.hint: Use SELECT c.CustomerName, o.OrderID FROM Customers c INNER JOIN Orders o ON c.CustomerID = o.CustomerID;
exercises.solutionCode: SELECT Users.name, Orders.amount FROM Users JOIN Orders ON Users.id = Orders.user_id;

exercises.title: Subquery
exercises.description: Use a subquery to find customers whose CustomerID appears in the Orders table.
exercises.hint: Use WHERE CustomerID IN (SELECT CustomerID FROM Orders);
exercises.solutionCode: SELECT name FROM Users ORDER BY age DESC LIMIT 3;

exercises.title: CREATE TABLE & Complex Query
exercises.description: Create a table Products with columns: ProductID INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, ProductName TEXT, Price REAL. Insert 3 products and select all products where Price > 10 ordered by Price DESC.
exercises.starterCode: -- Create Products table, insert data, and query
exercises.hint: CREATE TABLE Products (...); INSERT INTO Products VALUES (...); SELECT * FROM Products WHERE Price > 10 ORDER BY Price DESC;
exercises.solutionCode: CREATE TABLE Products (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), price DECIMAL(10,2));
